meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Ship SaaS Faster
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your product roadmap. Learn to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to eliminate technical debt and ship your MVP with professional precision.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' syndrome is usually a technical debt bottleneck. By integrating Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for iterative debugging, you move from manual troubleshooting to automated velocity. This workflow forces modularity, ensures test coverage, and keeps you focused on shipping features rather than fixing syntax errors.
content.intro:
Your codebase is a graveyard of half-baked features and 'temporary' hacks. You started with the vision of a disruptive SaaS, but now you’re buried under a mountain of cryptic stack traces and broken dependencies. The dream isn't dying; your development workflow is just obsolete.

Professional founders don't debug manually—they architect systems that self-correct. By treating your codebase as a living, LLM-navigable graph, you can slash development time by 70%. It’s time to stop fighting the code and start orchestrating it.

steps.title: Map Your Technical Debt
steps.description: Identify the exact modules stalling your progress. Use AI to scan your repository and generate a dependency risk report.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase for high-complexity functions that lack unit tests. Provide a prioritized list of modules causing integration friction.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Points
steps.description: Don't debug the whole app. Use Claude Code to create a sandbox environment for the specific feature that won't ship.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimal reproduction script for the auth middleware failure that prevents user signup.

steps.title: Automated Refactoring
steps.description: Apply AI-driven refactoring to simplify complex logic that is prone to breaking, ensuring your code remains maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this module into a functional pattern to reduce side effects and increase testability.

steps.title: Write Context-Aware Tests
steps.description: Use your existing codebase context to generate tests that actually catch edge cases rather than just passing syntactically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate an integration test suite for the checkout flow, covering success, failure, and edge-case timeout scenarios.

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Verification
steps.description: Finalize your fix by running a dry-run deployment check to ensure no regressions were introduced.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a dependency impact analysis on the recent refactor. Will this update break the existing production API routes?

pro_tips: Always use a .claudeignore file to exclude build artifacts and node_modules to keep the AI context window focused on business logic.
Force the AI to provide a 'Step-by-Step Execution Plan' before it writes a single line of code to prevent hallucinated architectural changes.
Use 'Chain-of-Thought' prompting: ask the AI to explain the logic of the bug before asking for the fix to ensure it understands the root cause.
Treat your error logs as the primary prompt source; feed raw JSON logs into the AI rather than summarizing them yourself.
Maintain a 'Golden Path' test file that the AI must run after every single modification to guarantee that core revenue-generating features never break.
content.next_steps: Once your core flow is stable, integrate a CI/CD pipeline that triggers Claude Code to run regression tests on every pull request.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical debt dictate your launch date. Leverage the same workflows as top-tier engineers to ship faster and scale smarter. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
